audit_status.source_base: Hebrew Leviticus / Vayiqra guilt/purification offering text tradition, draft aligned later
audit_status.review_note: First-pass draft reviewed for specific guilt cases, sliding-scale offering access, confession, birds/flour options, holy-things misuse, and asham/guilt offering.

verses.restored: When a soul/life sins/misaligns, hearing voice of oath as witness, whether he saw or knew, if he does not tell, he shall bear his iniquity.
Or when a soul/life touches any unclean thing, whether carcass of unclean wild animal, unclean domestic animal, or unclean swarming thing, and it is hidden from him, and he is unclean and guilty,
or when he touches human uncleanness, any uncleanness by which one becomes unclean, and it is hidden from him, then he knows and becomes guilty,
or when a soul/life swears rashly with lips to do evil or to do good, whatever a human utters rashly with oath, and it is hidden from him, then he knows and becomes guilty in one of these,
it shall be when he becomes guilty in one of these, he shall confess what he sinned/misaligned concerning it.

He shall bring his guilt/reparation to YHWH for his sin/misalignment that he sinned: a female from flock, lamb or goat, for sin/misalignment offering, and priest shall make atonement/covering for him from his sin/misalignment.

If his hand does not reach enough for sheep/goat, he shall bring as his guilt/reparation for what he sinned two turtledoves or two sons of pigeon to YHWH, one for sin/misalignment offering and one for burnt offering.
He shall bring them to priest, and priest shall bring near first that which is for sin/misalignment offering, pinch its head at back of neck but not divide it.
He shall sprinkle from blood/life of sin/misalignment offering on side of altar, and remaining blood/life shall be drained at base of altar; it is sin/misalignment offering.
The second he shall make burnt offering according to judgment. Priest shall make atonement/covering for him from his sin/misalignment that he sinned, and it shall be forgiven him.

If his hand does not reach two turtledoves or two sons of pigeon, then he shall bring as his offering for what he sinned a tenth ephah of fine flour for sin/misalignment offering; he shall not put oil on it nor give frankincense on it, for it is sin/misalignment offering.
He shall bring it to priest, and priest shall grasp from it full handful as memorial portion and turn it into smoke on altar upon fire-offerings of YHWH; it is sin/misalignment offering.
Priest shall make atonement/covering for him over his sin/misalignment that he sinned in one of these, and it shall be forgiven him; it shall belong to priest like grain offering.

When a soul/life acts unfaithfully and sins/misaligns in error/unintentionally regarding holy things of YHWH, he shall bring his guilt/reparation to YHWH: a complete ram from flock, by your valuation in silver shekels according to sanctuary shekel, for guilt offering.

What he sinned/misaligned from holy thing he shall repay, and add fifth to it, and give it to priest. Priest shall make atonement/covering for him with ram of guilt offering, and it shall be forgiven him.
If a soul/life sins/misaligns and does one from any commands of YHWH that should not be done, though he did not know, he is guilty and shall bear his iniquity.

He shall bring a complete ram from flock, by your valuation, for guilt offering to priest; priest shall make atonement/covering for him for error that he erred and did not know, and it shall be forgiven him.
It is guilt/reparation offering; guilty, he was guilty before YHWH.

translation_notes: Chapter includes confession in v.5: repair includes truthful naming.
Sliding scale access is crucial: flock, birds, or flour; even the poor have a ritual path.
Asham/guilt-reparation offering includes restitution plus added fifth when holy things are misused.
Unclean does not mean morally disgusting; it is ritual-state language requiring careful anti-shame framing.
safety_note: Do not use clean/unclean language for body shame, disability stigma, or disgust toward people. Leviticus 5 offers accessible repair paths and scaled offerings according to means.
